chunk_summary: This section addresses therapeutic oral iron regimens for adolescent anemia linked to menarche, emphasizing adherence, follow-up, and non-response work-up strategies.
learning_objectives: Understand therapeutic oral iron regimens for mild/moderate anemia in post-menarche adolescents.
Learn to operationally define and investigate poor response to iron therapy.
Identify key factors such as adherence, ongoing losses, and alternative diagnoses in anemia management.
key_facts: Severe anemia (Hb <8 g/dL) with hemodynamic compromise requires urgent referral.
Therapeutic regimen: 2 IFA tablets daily for 3 months (60 mg elemental iron + 500 mcg folic acid per tablet).
Expected hemoglobin rise: 1–2 g/dL within 2–4 weeks of adherence.
Poor response is defined as <1 g/dL rise in Hb after 2–4 weeks of therapy.
Investigate non-response: adherence, HMB, occult bleeding, hemoglobinopathy, malabsorption, or inflammation.
reference_quote: Prescribe two IFA tablets daily for 3 months for mild/moderate anemia (each tablet typically contains 60 mg elemental iron plus 500 mcg folic acid).
document_key_topics: Therapeutic oral iron regimens for adolescent anemia
